hero.title: Quantum BTC
hero.subtitle: The Ultimate Lightning Network Stress-Test
hero.description: Not a game of chance. A provably fair cryptographic experiment funding the future of Bitcoin's post-quantum security.

manifesto.title: The Quantum Manifesto
manifesto.content: Satoshi Nakamoto's vision requires defense against tomorrow's threats. We are using micro-transactions to stress-test the Lightning Network, while funding research into quantum-resistant cryptography.

l1_l2.title: L1 Vault vs L2 Pocket
l1_l2.l1: Bitcoin Layer 1 is the vault. Slow, expensive, but immutable.
l1_l2.l2: Lightning Network is your pocket wallet. Instant, extremely cheap, perfect for everyday use.

wallets.title: Recommended Lightning Wallets
wallets.muun: Good for beginners. Beware of high hidden fees if the main network (L1) is congested.
wallets.phoenix: Excellent non-custodial wallet. Perfect for users in emerging markets.
wallets.wos: Custodial but easiest for immediate onboarding. Only store small amounts to test the network.

faq.title: Security FAQ
faq.a1: No. This is a stress-taste mechanism designed to extract verifiable entropy and fund open-source research.
faq.a2: We use the Drand network for provable, verifiable randomness. The hash is transparent.

quantum_shield.title: Sovereignty in the Quantum Era: Your Roadmap
quantum_shield.intro: Quantum computing is not just sci-fi; it's the biggest technical challenge Bitcoin will face in the next decade. At Quantum BTC, our mission is to ensure Satoshi's legacy survives this transition.
quantum_shield.desc1: Bitcoin uses ECDSA for digital signatures. A sufficiently powerful quantum computer, using Shor's algorithm, could compute your private key from your public key in seconds.
quantum_shield.bullet1_1: If you use modern addresses (Native SegWit / Bech32), your public key remains hidden behind a hash, which is more resistant to quantum attacks.
quantum_shield.bullet1_2: Vulnerability: Ancient addresses starting with '1' and addresses that have already spent funds are at direct risk.
quantum_shield.bip360: BIP-360: Lattice Signatures. Offers solid security but inflates transaction sizes.
quantum_shield.commit: Commit-then-Reveal. Efficient emergency exit, but slow for daily usage.
quantum_shield.title3: 3. Our Strategy: Guided Migration with Covenants
quantum_shield.strategy: Through Covenants (Spending Restrictions) and Timelocks, we support tools for a forced yet secure migration.
quantum_shield.step3: Experiment with the Lightning Network to reduce L1 exposure.
quantum_shield.step4: Contribute to the stress-test to directly fund this research.
quantum_shield.conclusion: Security is not a state, it's a process. By testing the Lightning Network, you protect the protocol.
